papers.title: Your UnEmbedding Matrix is Secretly a Feature Lens for Text Embeddings
papers.abstract: Abstract Text embeddings from large language models are enhanced by EmbedFilter, a linear transformation that reduces the influence of high-frequency tokens and improves semantic representations while enabling dimensionality reduction.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Large language models exhibit impressive zero-shot capabilities across a wide range of downstream tasks. However, they struggle to function as off-the-shelf embedding models, leading to suboptimal performance on massive text embedding benchmarks. In this paper, we identify a potential cause underlying this deficiency. Our motivation stems from an unexpected observation: text embeddings tend to align with frequent but uninformative tokens when projected onto the vocabulary space. We argue that this excessive expression of high-frequency tokens suppresses the model's ability to capture nuanced semantics. To address this, we introduce EmbedFilter, a simple linear transformation designed to refine text embeddings derived from LLMs directly. Specifically, we uncover that the unembedding matrix within LLMs encodes a latent space that is actively writing these frequent tokens into embedding space. By filtering out this subspace, EmbedFilter suppress the influence of high-frequency tokens, thereby enhancing semantic representations. As a compelling byproduct, this enables an inherent dimensionality reduction, lowering index storage and speedup retrieval while fully preserving the refined embedding quality. Our experiments across multiple LLM backbones demonstrate that LLMs equipped with EmbedFilter achieve superior zero-shot downstream performance even with significantly reduced embedding dimensions. We hope our findings provide deeper insights into the mechanisms of LLM-based representations and inspire more principled designs to improve text embeddings training. Our code is available at https://github.com/CentreChen/EmbFilter.
affiliations: Gaoling School of Artificial Intelligence, Renmin University of China

ru.title: Фильтрация частых токенов для улучшения семантики эмбеддингов
ru.desc: В работе предложен метод EmbedFilter — линейное преобразование, которое улучшает качество текстовых эмбеддингов, получаемых из больших языковых моделей. Авторы выявили, что эмбеддинги чрезмерно выражают высокочастотные, но малоинформативные токены, что подавляет способность модели захватывать семантические нюансы. EmbedFilter фильтрует подпространство, созданное матрицей анэмбеддинга LLM, подавляя влияние частых токенов и укрепляя представления смысла. Как дополнительный бонус, метод обеспечивает естественное снижение размерности эмбеддингов без потери качества, что ускоряет поиск и снижает требования к памяти.

en.title: Enhancing Text Embeddings by Filtering High-Frequency Tokens
en.desc: This paper introduces EmbedFilter, a linear transformation that enhances text embeddings from large language models (LLMs) by reducing the impact of high-frequency tokens. The authors identify that these frequent tokens can obscure the model's ability to capture more subtle semantic meanings, leading to poorer performance in embedding tasks. By filtering out the influence of these high-frequency tokens, EmbedFilter improves the quality of the embeddings while also allowing for dimensionality reduction. The results show that LLMs using EmbedFilter achieve better performance on various downstream tasks, even with smaller embedding sizes.

zh.title: 提升文本嵌入质量的EmbedFilter
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种名为EmbedFilter的线性变换，用于增强大型语言模型生成的文本嵌入。通过减少高频词的影响，EmbedFilter改善了语义表示，并实现了维度的降低。研究发现，文本嵌入在词汇空间中往往与频繁但信息量低的词对齐，这抑制了模型捕捉细微语义的能力。实验表明，使用EmbedFilter的模型在零-shot任务上表现优越，即使嵌入维度显著减少。

papers.title: SoCRATES: Towards Reliable Automated Evaluation of Proactive LLM Mediation across Domains and Socio-cognitive Variations
papers.abstract: Abstract SoCRATES presents a realistic multi-domain benchmark for evaluating proactive LLM mediators across various socio-cognitive adaptation axes, demonstrating that even top-performing models only resolve about one-third of the consensus gap in conflict resolution.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Evaluating LLM mediators remains challenging, as mediation unfolds as a real-time trajectory shaped by disputants' shifting emotions, intentions, and context. Existing testbeds rely on a few expert-authored domains, vary mainly strategic posture, and score every turn against every topic, introducing off-topic noise. We introduce SoCRATES, a benchmark for evaluating proactive LLM mediators in realistic, multi-domain testbeds. It constructs scenarios from real conflicts through an agentic pipeline across eight domains, probes five socio-cognitive adaptation axes (strategic posture, party composition, history length, emotional reactivity, and cultural identity), and scores each topic only on the turns that advance it via a topic-localized evaluator. The evaluator reaches 0.82 alignment with human experts, more than doubling a per-turn baseline. Benchmarking eight frontier LLMs, we find that even the strongest mediator closes only about a third of the unmediated consensus gap under diverse and realistic testbeds, with performance varying sharply by socio-cognitive axis, highlighting that progress lies in social adaptation to diverse conditions.

ru.title: Социальная адаптация — ключ к медиации больших языковых моделей
ru.desc: В работе представлен бенчмарк SoCRATES для оценки способности LLM-моделей выступать в роли медиаторов в разрешении конфликтов на основе реальных сценариев из восьми различных областей. Бенчмарк проверяет пять осей социально-когнитивной адаптации: стратегическую позицию, состав сторон, длину истории взаимодействия, эмоциональную реактивность и культурную идентичность. Авторы создали специализированный оценщик с достижением 0.82 согласованности с экспертными суждениями, который оценивает релевантные к теме ходы диалога отдельно. Исследование показало, что даже лучшие современные модели закрывают только около трети промежутка в достижении консенсуса, демонстрируя критическую важность социальной адаптации для успешной медиации.

en.title: SoCRATES: Advancing LLM Mediation through Realistic Benchmarking
en.desc: SoCRATES is a new benchmark designed to evaluate proactive large language model (LLM) mediators in various real-world conflict scenarios. It focuses on five socio-cognitive adaptation axes, such as emotional reactivity and cultural identity, to assess how well these models can mediate disputes. The benchmark shows that even the best LLM mediators only manage to close about one-third of the consensus gap in conflict resolution, indicating significant room for improvement. By using a topic-localized evaluator that aligns closely with human expert judgments, SoCRATES aims to provide a more accurate measure of mediation effectiveness across diverse contexts.

zh.title: SoCRATES：评估调解者的新基准
zh.desc: SoCRATES是一个用于评估主动大型语言模型（LLM）调解者的多领域基准测试。该基准通过真实冲突构建场景，考察五个社会认知适应维度，展示了即使是表现最好的模型在解决冲突时也只能弥补大约三分之一的共识差距。现有的测试平台主要依赖少数专家撰写的领域，容易引入与主题无关的噪声。SoCRATES通过局部评估器对每个主题进行评分，显著提高了与人类专家的一致性。

papers.title: GENEB: Why Genomic Models Are Hard to Compare
papers.abstract: Abstract GENEB presents a comprehensive benchmark for evaluating genomic foundation models across diverse tasks and architectures under a unified protocol.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Progress in genomic foundation models is difficult to assess due to fragmented benchmarks, incompatible evaluation protocols, and task-specific reporting. As a result, claims of superiority or generality across models are often not directly comparable. We introduce GENEB, a large-scale diagnostic benchmark that evaluates frozen representations from 40 genomic foundation models across 100 tasks spanning 13 functional categories under a unified probing-based protocol, including few-shot regimes. GENEB enables controlled comparison across model scale, architecture, tokenization, and pretraining data while explicitly exposing task-level trade-offs. Our analysis shows that aggregate leaderboards are unstable: model rankings vary sharply across task categories, scale provides only modest and inconsistent gains, and architectural and pretraining alignment frequently outweigh parameter count. These results highlight limitations of current evaluation practices and position GENEB as a reference framework for principled comparison and category-aware model selection in genomic machine learning.
affiliations: Moscow Independent Research Institute of Artificial Intelligence, Moscow, Russia
Moscow State Institute of Steel and Alloys, Moscow, Russia

ru.title: Единый стандарт оценки геномных фундаментальных моделей
ru.desc: GENEB — это комплексный бенчмарк для оценки геномных фундаментальных моделей на основе единого протокола тестирования. Исследователи разработали диагностический набор данных, который оценивает замороженные представления 40 различных моделей на 100 задачах, охватывающих 13 функциональных категорий, включая режимы с малым количеством примеров. Анализ результатов показал, что агрегированные лидерборды нестабильны, а ранжирование моделей существенно варьируется в зависимости от категории задач. GENEB служит эталонной базой для объективного сравнения геномных моделей и помогает учитывать специфику различных типов задач при выборе архитектур и методов обучения.

en.title: GENEB: A Unified Benchmark for Genomic Model Evaluation
en.desc: GENEB is a new benchmark designed to evaluate genomic foundation models in a consistent way across various tasks and architectures. It addresses the challenges of fragmented benchmarks and incompatible evaluation methods that make it hard to compare different models. By assessing 40 genomic models on 100 tasks, GENEB allows researchers to see how models perform under a unified protocol, including few-shot learning scenarios. The findings reveal that model rankings can change significantly based on task categories, and factors like architecture and pretraining data often matter more than just the number of parameters.

zh.title: GENEB：基因组模型评估的新标准
zh.desc: GENEB是一个全面的基准测试，用于评估基因组基础模型在不同任务和架构下的表现。它解决了现有基准测试碎片化、评估协议不兼容和任务特定报告的问题。通过统一的探测协议，GENEB能够对40个基因组基础模型在100个任务中的表现进行比较。我们的分析表明，当前的评估实践存在局限性，GENEB为基因组机器学习中的模型选择提供了一个参考框架。

papers.title: MMAE: A Massive Multitask Audio Editing Benchmark
papers.abstract: Abstract MMAE presents a comprehensive benchmark for instruction-based audio editing across multiple modalities and complexity levels, revealing significant gaps in current model capabilities.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct We introduce MMAE, a Massive Multitask Audio Editing benchmark, serving as the first comprehensive evaluation testbed designed for general-purpose instruction-based audio editing. Spurred by the shift toward intelligent creation, interactive editing has rapidly expanded from visual domains, pioneered by models like Nano-banana 2 for images and Gemini-Omni for video, into audio. However, the current evaluation infrastructure lags severely, remaining highly fragmented and restricted to specific subdomains or basic operations. Unlike existing benchmarks that are limited in scope, MMAE extends to a broad spectrum of real-world scenarios, encompassing 7 distinct audio modalities, including sound, speech, music, and their mixtures. Furthermore, we establish a comprehensive taxonomy spanning 6 levels of task complexity, from basic modifications to multi-hop reasoning and multi-round editing, 2 levels of granularity, and 8 distinct operation types. Meticulously curated through human-agent collaboration, MMAE comprises 2,000 high-fidelity samples paired with a pioneering rubric-based evaluation framework. By decomposing free-form tasks into 17,741 verifiable criteria, this robust rubric-based paradigm enables a precise, multi-dimensional assessment of both instruction following and context consistency. Our extensive evaluation of leading models reveals that current systems remain far from achieving reliable edits. Strikingly, the Exact Match Rate (EMR) consistently falls below 5% and plummets to an absolute 0% in complex, mixed-modality tasks, exposing critical bottlenecks in precise execution and structural robustness. We hope MMAE will serve as a catalyst for future advances in the intelligent creation community, providing a clear diagnostic roadmap and establishing a standardized, long-lasting evaluation paradigm for next-generation audio editing systems.

ru.title: Бенчмарк для выявления слабых мест в моделях редактирования аудио
ru.desc: Представлена MMAE — первый комплексный бенчмарк для оценки моделей, которые редактируют аудио по текстовым инструкциям. Бенчмарк охватывает 7 различных типов аудиоданных (звуки, речь, музыку и их смеси) и включает 2000 образцов высокого качества с разными уровнями сложности редактирования. Авторы разработали инновационную рубрик-базированную систему оценки, разбивающую задачи на 17741 проверяемый критерий для точной оценки следования инструкциям. Исследование показало критические проблемы в существующих моделях: точность выполнения (EMR) падает ниже 5%, а для сложных смешанных задач составляет 0%.

en.title: MMAE: Bridging the Gap in Audio Editing Evaluation
en.desc: The paper introduces MMAE, a benchmark for evaluating instruction-based audio editing across various modalities and complexities. It highlights the limitations of current models in performing reliable audio edits, particularly in complex tasks involving multiple audio types. MMAE includes a comprehensive taxonomy of tasks and a detailed rubric for assessment, allowing for precise evaluation of model performance. The findings indicate that existing systems struggle significantly, with low success rates in executing edits, underscoring the need for improved methodologies in audio editing.

zh.title: MMAE：音频编辑的未来基准
zh.desc: MMAE是一个全面的基准测试，专注于基于指令的音频编辑，涵盖多种模态和复杂性水平。它揭示了当前模型能力的显著差距，尤其是在复杂的混合模态任务中，准确匹配率低于5%。MMAE包括2000个高保真样本，并通过17,741个可验证标准进行评估，提供了多维度的评估框架。我们希望MMAE能推动智能创作领域的进步，建立一个标准化的音频编辑系统评估范式。

papers.title: AnchorWorld: Embodied Egocentric World Simulation with View-based Evolution Customization
papers.abstract: Abstract AnchorWorld advances egocentric simulation through enhanced interaction integrity and flexible world customization using 3D human motion and anchor view definitions.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Despite being a pivotal frontier, interactive world modeling remains underexplored in terms of the versatile controllability required by practical scenarios. To bridge this gap, we present AnchorWorld, a framework that advances egocentric simulation through enhanced interaction integrity and a flexible mechanism for world customization. First, we utilize 3D human motion as the primary interaction modality. To complement the out-of-view or truncated body parts in egocentric views, we introduce an auxiliary training supervision that incorporates exogenous viewpoints decoupled from the agent's first-person sensorium. It allows the model to observe the agent's full-body positioning relative to the environment, facilitating a more robust spatial grounding of human-world interactions. Furthermore, we propose a simple yet effective mechanism for customizing self-evolving worlds. This is achieved by defining anchor views within a unified world coordinate system, coupled with textual descriptions dictating the dynamic evolution of local scenes. Experimental results show that AnchorWorld significantly outperforms state-of-the-art baselines, while ablation studies validate the effectiveness of our key designs. Notably, our customization scheme exhibits promising spatio-temporal geometric consistency and adheres strictly to the prescribed evolutionary dynamics.

ru.title: Полнотелое управление виртуальным миром через якорные представления
ru.desc: AnchorWorld — это фреймворк для эгоцентрического моделирования, который использует трёхмерное движение человека как основной способ взаимодействия с окружением. Для решения проблемы скрытых или обрезанных частей тела в эгоцентрическом виде авторы вводят вспомогательное обучение с использованием внешних точек зрения. Предложен механизм кастомизации виртуальных миров путём определения опорных точек обзора в единой системе координат с текстовыми описаниями динамики сцен. Экспериментальные результаты показывают превосходство подхода над существующими методами с высокой геометрической согласованностью.

en.title: Revolutionizing Egocentric Simulation with AnchorWorld
en.desc: AnchorWorld is a new framework that improves egocentric simulation by allowing better interaction and customization of virtual environments. It uses 3D human motion as the main way for users to interact with the world, while also addressing limitations in viewing body parts from a first-person perspective. The framework introduces a training method that uses different viewpoints to help the model understand the full-body positioning of users in relation to their surroundings. Additionally, it features a unique system for customizing worlds that evolves based on defined anchor views and textual descriptions, leading to better performance compared to existing methods.

zh.title: AnchorWorld：增强自我中心模拟的创新框架
zh.desc: AnchorWorld 是一个增强自我中心模拟的框架，通过提高交互完整性和灵活的世界定制机制来实现。它利用 3D 人体运动作为主要的交互方式，并引入辅助训练监督，以补充自我中心视角中缺失的身体部分。该框架允许模型观察代理与环境的全身定位，从而增强人类与世界交互的空间基础。实验结果表明，AnchorWorld 在性能上显著优于现有的最先进基线，并且其定制方案在时空几何一致性方面表现出色。

papers.title: Direct 3D-Aware Object Insertion via Decomposed Visual Proxies
papers.abstract: Abstract DIFFUSION-BASED OBJECT INSERTION FRAMEWORK WITH POSE CONTROL THROUGH DECOMPOSED GUIDANCE COMPONENTS  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Object insertion aims to seamlessly composite a reference object into a specified region of a background image. Recent diffusion-based methods achieve high visual quality but formulate insertion as a simple 2D inpainting task, providing no explicit control over the object's 3D pose and limiting their practical applicability. We propose DIRECT (Decomposed Injection for Reference Composition and Target-integration), a novel framework that integrates interactive pose manipulation with high-fidelity 2D image synthesis to enable pose-controllable object insertion. Our method decomposes the insertion conditions into three complementary components: appearance guidance capturing visual details from the reference object, geometry guidance derived from the user-adjusted 3D proxy, and context guidance from the target background. By injecting them through separate pathways, DIRECT avoids feature entanglement and simultaneously preserves reference appearance, follows the user-specified pose, and adapts the object to the target scene. We also introduce an automated data construction pipeline to improve the diversity and quality of training data. Experiments show that DIRECT outperforms previous methods in both geometric controllability and visual quality.
affiliations: VCIP, School of Computer Science, Nankai University

ru.title: Управляемая вставка объектов через разложение компонент guidance
ru.desc: В работе предлагается фреймворк DIRECT для вставки объектов в изображения с контролем трёхмерной позы. В отличие от существующих методов на основе диффузионных моделей, которые рассматривают задачу как простой 2D inpainting, предложенный подход разделяет условия вставки на три компоненты: guidance по внешнему виду объекта, guidance по геометрии из 3D прокси и guidance по контексту фона. Каждая компонента проходит через отдельный канал в сети, что предотвращает смешивание признаков и обеспечивает одновременное сохранение внешнего вида, соответствие указанной позе и адаптацию к целевой сцене. Авторы также разработали автоматизированный конвейер построения датасета для улучшения разнообразия и качества обучающих данных.

en.title: Seamless Object Insertion with 3D Pose Control
en.desc: This paper presents DIRECT, a new framework for object insertion that allows users to control the 3D pose of objects in images. Unlike traditional methods that treat insertion as a simple 2D task, DIRECT uses three distinct guidance components: appearance, geometry, and context. This separation helps maintain the object's visual fidelity while adapting it to the background scene. The framework also includes an automated data pipeline to enhance the training dataset, leading to better performance in both pose control and image quality.

zh.title: 实现可控姿态的高质量物体插入
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种新的框架，名为DIRECT，旨在实现可控姿态的物体插入。该方法通过分解插入条件为外观引导、几何引导和上下文引导三个互补组件，来提高插入的质量和灵活性。DIRECT能够在保持参考物体外观的同时，遵循用户指定的3D姿态，并适应目标场景。实验结果表明，DIRECT在几何可控性和视觉质量方面优于以往的方法。

papers.title: Robots Need More than VLA and World Models
papers.abstract: Abstract Robot intelligence advancement requires integrating unstructured behavioral data through specialized interfaces for labeling, embodiment mapping, world modeling, and reward inference rather than relying solely on policy scaling.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Generalist robot intelligence is often framed as a policy-scaling problem: collect more robot demonstrations, train larger Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models, and expect broader generalisation. In this position paper, we argue that this framing is incomplete. The central bottleneck is not only policy learning, but the absence of mechanisms that convert the world's abundant unstructured behavioural data into grounded robot supervision. Human motion, internet video, simulation rollouts, and interactive demonstrations contain rich information about tasks, goals, contacts, failures, and physical constraints, yet most of this information is not directly usable by robot policies because it lacks embodiment-specific action labels, task semantics, and reward structure. We identify four missing components for the next generation of robotics: data interfaces for autolabelling unstructured behaviour, embodiment interfaces for retargeting human motion to robot actions, world-model interfaces for physics-grounded 3D reasoning, and reward interfaces for inferring task progress and success from video and language. We survey recent progress in robot foundation models, cross-embodiment datasets, learning from video, world models, and reward modelling, and propose a research agenda for building robotics systems that can learn not only from robot demonstrations, but from the broader physical world.

ru.title: От масштабирования политик к интерпретации мира: четыре интерфейса для интеллектуальных роботов
ru.desc: В статье утверждается, что развитие робототехники требует не просто масштабирования политик обучения (policy scaling), а интеграции неструктурированных данных о поведении через специализированные интерфейсы. Авторы выделяют четыре критические компоненты: интерфейсы для автоматической разметки поведения, интерфейсы воплощения для преобразования человеческих движений в робототехнические действия, интерфейсы мировых моделей для физического 3D-рассуждения, и интерфейсы вознаграждения для инференса прогресса задач. Исследование охватывает фундаментальные модели робототехники, кроссемодальные датасеты, обучение из видео и моделирование вознаграждений. Предлагается новая парадигма, позволяющая роботам учиться не только из демонстраций, но и из более широкого физического мира.

en.title: Unlocking Robot Intelligence: Beyond Policy Scaling
en.desc: This paper discusses the need for robots to learn from unstructured behavioral data instead of just relying on collecting more demonstrations and training larger models. It highlights that the main challenge is not just improving policy learning, but also developing methods to convert rich, unstructured data into useful supervision for robots. The authors identify four key components that are missing in current robotics: data interfaces for autolabeling, embodiment interfaces for mapping human actions to robot actions, world-model interfaces for understanding physics, and reward interfaces for assessing task success. They propose a research agenda to enhance robot learning by leveraging diverse sources of information from the physical world.

zh.title: 机器人智能发展的新视角
zh.desc: 本论文探讨了机器人智能发展的新方法，强调需要通过专门的接口整合非结构化的行为数据，而不仅仅依赖于政策扩展。我们认为，当前的政策学习框架不够完整，缺乏将丰富的非结构化行为数据转化为机器人监督的机制。人类运动、互联网视频和互动演示中蕴含着丰富的信息，但由于缺乏具体的动作标签和任务语义，这些信息无法直接用于机器人政策。我们提出了四个关键组件，以推动下一代机器人技术的发展，包括自动标记非结构化行为的数据接口、将人类运动重定向到机器人动作的接口、基于物理的3D推理的世界模型接口，以及从视频和语言中推断任务进展和成功的奖励接口。

papers.title: When Tools Fail: Benchmarking Dynamic Replanning and Anomaly Recovery in LLM Agents
papers.abstract: Abstract ToolMaze benchmark reveals that real-world tool failures significantly degrade TIR performance, with implicit semantic failures causing the most severe drops and dynamic replanning emerging as a key bottleneck.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Existing benchmarks evaluate Tool-Integrated Reasoning (TIR) in LLMs on idealized ''happy paths'', largely overlooking real-world tool failures. We introduce ToolMaze, a benchmark for dynamic path discovery and error recovery in TIR agents. To separate systematic replanning from blind trial-and-error, ToolMaze adopts a two-dimensional design: DAG-based topological complexity and a 2 times 2 taxonomy of tool perturbations (explicit/implicit, transient/permanent). Evaluations show that perturbations degrade performance across nearly all models, with the sharpest drops under implicit semantic failures. Driven by systemic over-trust in corrupted outputs, Perturbation Recovery Rate (PRR) plummets by around 37\% in these scenarios, while complex topologies trap agents in futile trial-and-error loops. Crucially, agentic fault-tolerance improves with model scale 3.66times slower than basic task execution, highlighting dynamic replanning as a distinct bottleneck unaddressed by model scaling or prompting. Data and code are available at https://github.com/Zhudongsheng75/ToolMaze.

ru.title: Когда инструменты ломаются: обучение агентов справляться с реальными отказами
ru.desc: В статье представлен бенчмарк ToolMaze для оценки способности языковых моделей работать с инструментами в условиях реальных отказов и ошибок. Исследование показывает, что существующие бенчмарки проверяют только идеальные сценарии, игнорируя проблемы, возникающие при сбоях инструментов, особенно неявных семантических ошибок. Авторы разработали двумерную схему оценки с учётом топологической сложности и различных типов возмущений инструментов, обнаружив, что агенты часто чрезмерно доверяют повреждённым выводам. Результаты демонстрируют, что динамическое переплаирование становится узким местом, которое не решается увеличением масштаба модели или улучшением промптов.

en.title: Navigating Real-World Challenges in Tool-Integrated Reasoning
en.desc: The ToolMaze benchmark highlights how real-world tool failures can severely impact the performance of Tool-Integrated Reasoning (TIR) in large language models (LLMs). It identifies implicit semantic failures as the most detrimental, leading to significant drops in performance. The benchmark features a two-dimensional design that distinguishes between systematic replanning and random trial-and-error, focusing on the complexity of tool interactions. Results indicate that while larger models show improved fault tolerance, they still struggle with dynamic replanning, which remains a critical bottleneck in TIR applications.

zh.title: ToolMaze：应对真实工具故障的动态路径发现
zh.desc: ToolMaze基准测试揭示了现实世界中的工具故障会显著降低工具集成推理（TIR）的性能，尤其是隐式语义故障造成的性能下降最为严重。现有基准主要评估理想化的“快乐路径”，忽视了真实环境中的工具故障。我们引入了ToolMaze，一个用于动态路径发现和错误恢复的基准，采用了基于有向无环图的拓扑复杂性和工具扰动的分类方法。评估结果表明，扰动会在几乎所有模型中降低性能，尤其是在隐式语义故障的情况下，模型的恢复率显著下降。

papers.title: OpenSkill: Open-World Self-Evolution for LLM Agents
papers.abstract: Abstract OpenSkill enables self-evolving agents to develop skills and verification signals from scratch using open-world resources without target-task supervision, achieving high automated performance across benchmarks.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Self-evolving agents requires adaptation after deployment, but existing approaches assume a usable learning loop, such as curated skills, successful trajectories, or verifier signals. Real open-world deployments may provide none of these, offering only a task prompt. In this work, we study open-world self-evolution, where an agent must build both its skills and its own verification signals from scratch, using open-world resources but no target-task supervision. We propose OpenSkill, a framework that bootstraps this loop: it acquires grounded knowledge and verification anchors from documentation, repositories, and the web, synthesizes them into transferable skills, and refines those skills against self-built virtual tasks grounded in the anchors rather than in target answers. The open world thus supplies both the knowledge to be learned and a supervision-independent practice environment, with target-task supervision reserved for final evaluation. Across three benchmarks and two target agents, OpenSkill attains the best automated pass rate while satisfying the no-supervision constraint. Analysis shows its skills transfer across models without model-specific adaptation, and its self-built verifier aligns with ground-truth outcomes despite never accessing them.
affiliations: Massachusetts General Hospital and Harvard Medical School

ru.title: Агент, обучающий сам себя из ресурсов открытого мира
ru.desc: OpenSkill — это фреймворк для самоэволюционирующих агентов, которые способны самостоятельно разрабатывать навыки и сигналы верификации без надзора за целевой задачей, используя открытые ресурсы из интернета, документации и репозиториев. Система загружает знания из открытого мира, синтезирует их в передаваемые навыки и совершенствует эти навыки на искусственно созданных виртуальных задачах, которые не требуют доступа к правильным ответам. Разработанный верификатор на основе якорей из открытых ресурсов хорошо коррелирует с реальными результатами, несмотря на отсутствие доступа к целевой разметке. Подход достигает наилучшей автоматизированной производительности на нескольких бенчмарках и демонстрирует способность навыков переноситься между разными моделями без модель-специфической адаптации.

en.title: Empowering Agents to Evolve Skills Independently in an Open World
en.desc: OpenSkill is a framework designed for self-evolving agents that can learn and verify skills independently in an open-world environment without needing specific task supervision. It allows agents to gather knowledge from various resources like documentation and the web, creating transferable skills and verification signals from scratch. This approach enables agents to practice and refine their skills using self-generated virtual tasks, rather than relying on predefined answers. The results show that OpenSkill achieves high performance across benchmarks while maintaining the ability to transfer skills between different models without needing tailored adaptations.

zh.title: 开放世界中的自我进化智能体
zh.desc: OpenSkill 是一个框架，允许自我进化的智能体在没有目标任务监督的情况下，从开放世界资源中开发技能和验证信号。该方法通过从文档、代码库和网络中获取知识，合成可转移的技能，并在自建的虚拟任务中进行技能的精炼。OpenSkill 在三个基准测试和两个目标智能体上表现出色，达到了最佳的自动通过率，同时满足无监督的要求。分析表明，其技能可以在不同模型间转移，而自建的验证器与真实结果一致，尽管从未接触过这些结果。

papers.title: SubtleMemory: A Benchmark for Fine-Grained Relational Memory Discrimination in Long-Horizon AI Agents
papers.abstract: Abstract SubtleMemory benchmark evaluates AI agents' ability to handle complex relational memory structures that emerge during prolonged interactions, revealing limitations in current memory systems for preserving and utilizing nuanced memory relationships.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Persistent AI assistants, such as OpenClaw, accumulate large collections of related memories over long-term interactions. As these memories grow, they may reinforce one another, diverge across contexts, or directly conflict, making correct assistance depend on memory relations rather than isolated recall. Existing long-term memory benchmarks rarely probe how agents preserve and utilize such relations during downstream tasks. To address this gap, we introduce SubtleMemory, a benchmark for fine-grained relational memory discrimination in long-running AI agents. SubtleMemory constructs relation-controlled latent semantic artifacts whose variants instantiate complementary, nuanced, or contradictory relations, and embeds them into realistic user-agent histories, requiring agents to recover distributed relational structures during later queries and instructions. The benchmark contains 1,522 evaluation instances over 10 long histories, grounded in 1,090 relation-controlled memory-variant sets and spanning user-related and non-user-related queries. Evaluating six standalone memory systems, two Claw-style agents with native memory modules, and three Claw-style agents with plugin memory modules, we find that current systems remain weak on fine-grained relational memory discrimination. We further introduce diagnostic protocols that reveal distinct capability profiles across memory preservation, retrieval, and downstream reasoning stages.
affiliations: The Chinese University of Hong Kong

ru.title: Когда память забывает отношения: тест на понимание связей в долгосрочной памяти AI
ru.desc: Статья представляет бенчмарк SubtleMemory для оценки способности AI агентов работать со сложными отношениями между воспоминаниями, которые накапливаются во время длительного взаимодействия с пользователем. Авторы показывают, что существующие системы памяти плохо справляются с задачей различения тонких семантических связей между связанными воспоминаниями, когда они могут дополнять, противоречить или уточнять друг друга. Бенчмарк содержит 1522 примера на основе реалистичных историй взаимодействия пользователя и агента, где требуется восстановление распределённых реляционных структур при обработке новых запросов. Диагностические протоколы выявляют слабые места на всех этапах работы с памятью: сохранение информации, её извлечение и использование при рассуждении.

en.title: Enhancing AI Memory: The SubtleMemory Challenge
en.desc: The SubtleMemory benchmark assesses how well AI agents manage complex memory relationships during extended interactions. It highlights the shortcomings of existing memory systems in maintaining and utilizing nuanced memory connections. By creating realistic scenarios with varied relational contexts, the benchmark challenges agents to retrieve and apply these relationships effectively. The study reveals that current AI systems struggle with fine-grained memory discrimination, indicating a need for improved memory handling in long-term interactions.

zh.title: 评估AI代理的复杂关系记忆能力
zh.desc: SubtleMemory基准测试评估人工智能代理在长期交互中处理复杂关系记忆结构的能力。随着记忆的积累，这些记忆可能相互强化、在不同上下文中分歧或直接冲突，因此正确的辅助依赖于记忆关系而非孤立的回忆。现有的长期记忆基准测试很少探讨代理如何在下游任务中保持和利用这些关系。为了解决这个问题，我们引入了SubtleMemory基准，专注于细粒度的关系记忆辨别，评估现有系统在这一领域的表现。

papers.title: UniSHARP: Universal Sharp Monocular View Synthesis
papers.abstract: Abstract UniSHARP extends SHARP for universal monocular rendering across different camera systems by aligning images in an omnidirectional latent space through joint feature and Gaussian space alignment.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct In this work, we focus on extending SHARP, the popular photorealistic view synthesis method, for universal monocular rendering across a continuum of camera systems, from conventional perspective cameras to wide-field-of-view, fisheye and omnidirectional panoramic settings. To overcome the pinhole-specific assumptions of SHARP, our key idea is to align various images in a unified omnidirectional latent space. Thus, we propose UniSHARP, which performs implicit alignment in both feature and Gaussian spaces. Specifically, Gaussian primitives are arranged along rays and radial distances in a ray-based universal representation, while 2D semantic and 3D spatial features extracted from UniK3D-inspired encoders are jointly decoded to generate the complete Gaussian cloud. To comprehensively evaluate our method, we construct a benchmark covering diverse imaging systems across various scenes. The benchmark is further stratified by field of view (FoV) to enable fine-grained assessment of the universal monocular rendering task. Extensive experiments on the proposed benchmark demonstrate the effectiveness of UniSHARP, outperforming alternative methods by a large margin. The project page can be found at: https://insta360-research-team.github.io/Unisharp-website/

ru.title: Универсальный синтез видов для всех типов камер через омнидирекциональное пространство
ru.desc: UniSHARP расширяет метод SHARP для универсального монокулярного рендеринга изображений, полученных различными типами камер, от обычных до панорамных. Основная идея заключается в выравнивании изображений в единое омнидирекциональное латентное пространство путём согласования признаков и примитивов Гаусса. Примитивы Гаусса организованы вдоль лучей и радиальных расстояний в универсальном представлении на основе лучей, а семантические и пространственные признаки декодируются совместно для генерации полного облака Гаусса. Метод протестирован на новом бенчмарке с разнообразными системами визуализации и показывает значительное превосходство над альтернативными подходами.

en.title: UniSHARP: Universal Monocular Rendering for All Camera Types
en.desc: UniSHARP is an advanced method that enhances the SHARP technique for creating realistic images from a single camera view, applicable to various camera types. It achieves this by aligning images in a shared omnidirectional latent space, allowing for better integration of different camera perspectives. The method utilizes both feature alignment and Gaussian space alignment to generate a comprehensive representation of the scene. Extensive testing shows that UniSHARP significantly outperforms existing methods in universal monocular rendering across diverse imaging systems.

zh.title: UniSHARP：跨相机系统的单目渲染新方法
zh.desc: UniSHARP 是一种扩展 SHARP 的方法，旨在实现不同相机系统之间的单目渲染。它通过在一个统一的全向潜在空间中对图像进行对齐，克服了 SHARP 的针孔特定假设。该方法在特征和高斯空间中进行隐式对齐，生成完整的高斯云。通过构建一个涵盖多种成像系统的基准测试，UniSHARP 在单目渲染任务中表现出色，显著优于其他方法。

papers.title: LIMMT: Less is More for Motion Tracking
papers.abstract: Abstract Training with high-quality motion data improves tracking policy optimization trajectories, with minimal data subsets outperforming full datasets in physics-based humanoid motion tracking.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct We argue that high-quality motion data can steer tracking policies toward better optimization trajectories early in training. In this work, we introduce LIMMT (Less Is More for Motion Tracking). To our knowledge, this is the first data-centric study for physics-based humanoid motion tracking. We go beyond simply removing low-quality and erroneous clips, but define motion data quality through three dimensions: physics feasibility, diversity, and complexity. We show that even training with under 3% of AMASS yields better tracking performance than training with the full dataset. We further conduct data cleaning on the estimated web-sourced mocap data. Extensive experiments and analyses validate the effectiveness of our framework.

ru.title: Меньше данных — больше качества в отслеживании движений
ru.desc: В статье представлен подход LIMMT, который демонстрирует, что высококачественные данные движения могут улучшить обучение политики отслеживания движения человеческого тела в физических симуляторах. Авторы определяют качество данных через три измерения: физическую осуществимость, разнообразие и сложность движений. Эксперименты показывают, что обучение на менее чем 3% набора данных AMASS дает лучшие результаты, чем использование полного датасета. Работа впервые применяет центрированный на данных подход к задаче физического отслеживания движений человекоподобных персонажей.

en.title: Less Data, Better Motion Tracking!
en.desc: This paper presents LIMMT, a novel approach to improve motion tracking in physics-based humanoid models by utilizing high-quality motion data. The authors demonstrate that training with a small, carefully selected subset of data can lead to better optimization trajectories than using a larger, less curated dataset. They define motion data quality through three key dimensions: physics feasibility, diversity, and complexity, which guide the selection process. Their experiments show that using less than 3% of the AMASS dataset can outperform training with the entire dataset, highlighting the importance of data quality over quantity.

zh.title: 高质量数据，优化运动跟踪！
zh.desc: 本论文探讨了高质量运动数据在物理基础的人形运动跟踪中的重要性。我们提出了LIMMT（少即是多的运动跟踪），强调在训练初期使用高质量数据可以更好地优化跟踪策略。研究表明，即使只使用不到3%的AMASS数据，跟踪性能也优于使用完整数据集。我们还对网络来源的运动捕捉数据进行了清理，实验结果验证了我们框架的有效性。

papers.title: Watch, Remember, Reason: Human-View Video Understanding with MLLMs
papers.abstract: Abstract Multimodal large language models for video understanding are structured around three core capabilities—watching, remembering, and reasoning—with applications spanning multiple video domains and addressing challenges in perception, memory, and reasoning.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Video understanding is being rapidly transformed by multimodal large language models (MLLMs), as research moves from short clips to long, multimodal, and knowledge-intensive video scenarios. These scenarios require models to handle sparse evidence, long-range dependencies, multimodal alignment, and reliable inference under limited computational budgets. This work presents a human-view perspective on LLM-based video understanding, organized around three functional abilities: watching, remembering, and reasoning. Rather than treating video tasks as isolated benchmarks, this view provides a unified structure for analyzing how video MLLMs acquire evidence, preserve context, and produce grounded outputs. We introduce a formulation that characterizes video understanding systems by their perceptual representations, memory states, reasoning traces, and final predictions. Based on this formulation, we identify challenges in spatio-temporal perception, efficient long-video processing, memory modeling, streaming understanding, and faithful reasoning. Representative methods are organized by their roles in video MLLM systems. Watching covers fine-grained, comprehensive, audio-visual, and efficient perception. Remembering includes offline and streaming memory, while reasoning covers text-only reasoning and thinking with videos. We further examine application domains such as egocentric, sports, instructional, medical, and narrative videos, and cover training datasets and evaluation benchmarks across task types, supervision formats, modalities, and capability dimensions. Finally, we outline open problems and future directions for scalable, memory-aware, and evidence-grounded video intelligence. Related works will be continuously traced at https://github.com/marinero4972/Awesome-HumanView-VideoUnderstanding.
affiliations: School of Intelligence Science and Technology, Peking University

ru.title: Видео понимание через три столпа: восприятие, память и рассуждение
ru.desc: В статье рассматривается применение мультимодальных больших языковых моделей (MLLM) для понимания видео, организованное вокруг трёх ключевых способностей: восприятие, сохранение контекста и логический вывод. Авторы предлагают единую структуру для анализа видео-MLLM систем через призму перцепционных представлений, состояний памяти, трасс рассуждений и финальных предсказаний. В работе выделены основные вызовы: спatio-временное восприятие, эффективная обработка длинных видео, моделирование памяти и верные логические выводы. Исследование охватывает различные приложения (эгоцентричные, спортивные, медицинские видео) и включает анализ наборов данных, бенчмарков и направлений развития видео-интеллекта.

en.title: Empowering Video Understanding with Multimodal Large Language Models
en.desc: This paper discusses how multimodal large language models (MLLMs) can improve video understanding by focusing on three main abilities: watching, remembering, and reasoning. It highlights the challenges these models face, such as processing long videos, managing memory, and making accurate inferences from sparse data. The authors propose a framework to analyze video understanding systems based on their perceptual representations and reasoning processes. They also explore various application areas and suggest future research directions to enhance video intelligence.

zh.title: 多模态视频理解的三大能力：观看、记忆与推理
zh.desc: 这篇论文探讨了多模态大型语言模型（MLLMs）在视频理解中的应用，强调了观看、记忆和推理三大核心能力。研究表明，随着视频场景的复杂性增加，模型需要处理稀疏证据、长距离依赖和多模态对齐等挑战。论文提出了一种统一的框架，帮助分析视频MLLM如何获取证据、保持上下文和生成可靠的输出。最后，作者指出了在时空感知、长视频处理和记忆建模等方面的挑战，并展望了未来的发展方向。

papers.title: dots.tts Technical Report
papers.abstract: Abstract A 2B-parameter continuous autoregressive text-to-speech model trained on a multilingual corpus achieves state-of-the-art performance on multiple benchmarks while enabling efficient low-latency speech generation through specialized distillation techniques.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct We present dots.tts, a 2B-parameter continuous autoregressive text-to-speech (TTS) foundation model that models speech in a continuous latent space. Compared with existing continuous autoregressive models, our key innovations are threefold. First, we train an AudioVAE with multiple objectives to build a semantically structured and prediction-friendly continuous speech space. Second, we use full-history conditioning in the flow-matching head to preserve long-range consistency and reduce drift during generation. Third, we apply reward-free self-corrective post-training to the flow-matching head to further improve robustness and acoustic quality. After being trained on a large-scale multilingual corpus, dots.tts achieves the best average performance on Seed-TTS-Eval, with WERs of 0.94%/1.30%/6.60% and SIM scores of 81.0/77.1/79.5 on the zh/en/zh-hard test sets, respectively. Across other benchmarks, dots.tts also consistently demonstrates open-source state-of-the-art performance, exhibiting strong generation stability, voice cloning ability, and emotional expressiveness. For efficient inference, we further apply CFG-aware MeanFlow distillation, enabling low-latency speech generation with first-packet latencies of 85/54 ms in output streaming and dual-streaming modes, respectively. To facilitate reproducible research and practical deployment, we release the training and inference code, together with the pretrained, post-trained, and MeanFlow-distilled checkpoints, under the Apache 2.0 license.

ru.title: Быстрая и точная речь из текста: многоязычная foundation model с низкой латентностью
ru.desc: Представлена модель dots.tts — большая автарегрессивная нейронная сеть с 2 миллиардами параметров для синтеза речи из текста, обученная на многоязычном корпусе. Ключевые инновации включают обучение AudioVAE с несколькими целевыми функциями для создания структурированного непрерывного пространства речи, использование полной истории контекста в flow-matching головке для сохранения долгосрочной консистентности и применение самокорректирующей постобучения без использования наград. Модель достигает лучшей производительности на бенчмарках с низкой ошибкой распознавания речи и использует дистилляцию MeanFlow для эффективного вывода с минимальной задержкой.

en.title: Revolutionizing Speech Generation with dots.tts
en.desc: The paper introduces dots.tts, a 2B-parameter continuous autoregressive text-to-speech model that excels in generating speech from text. It utilizes a novel AudioVAE to create a structured continuous speech space, enhancing prediction accuracy. The model incorporates full-history conditioning to maintain consistency over long sequences and employs self-corrective post-training to boost robustness and sound quality. Trained on a diverse multilingual dataset, dots.tts achieves top performance on various benchmarks, demonstrating low-latency generation and strong capabilities in voice cloning and emotional expressiveness.

zh.title: 突破性的多语言文本到语音模型
zh.desc: 本文介绍了一种名为dots.tts的2B参数连续自回归文本到语音（TTS）模型，该模型在多语言语料库上训练，达到了多个基准测试的最先进性能。该模型的创新之处在于：首先，使用多目标训练的AudioVAE构建语义结构化的连续语音空间；其次，在流匹配头中使用全历史条件，以保持长距离一致性并减少生成过程中的漂移；最后，应用无奖励自我校正后训练，进一步提高了模型的鲁棒性和音质。经过大规模多语言语料库的训练，dots.tts在Seed-TTS-Eval上取得了最佳平均性能，并在其他基准测试中也表现出色，展现了强大的生成稳定性、声音克隆能力和情感表现力。

papers.title: Thinking with Imagination: Agentic Visual Spatial Reasoning with World Simulators
papers.abstract: Abstract Astra is an agentic spatial reasoning framework that enhances Vision-Language Models with action-conditioned visual imagination by coupling a reinforcement learning-trained policy with a world simulator for generating novel-view observations.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct While Vision-Language Models (VLMs) have shown strong visual reasoning capabilities, their spatial reasoning abilities remain largely constrained to the observed images and text-oriented chain-of-thought. They often struggle to infer unobserved layouts, maintain cross-view consistency, and reason from alternative viewpoints when only limited egocentric observations are available. In this work, we study this problem as thinking with imagination, where a VLM actively acquires imagined visual evidence by interacting with a world simulator during reasoning. We propose Astra, an agentic spatial reasoning framework that empowers VLMs with action-conditioned visual imagination. Specifically, Astra couples Astra-VL, an RL-trained VLM policy, with Astra-WM, a Bagel-based world simulator that generates novel-view observations from context images and natural-language camera motions. To provide reliable imagined evidence, Astra-WM is trained with view consistency tuning to improve pose and content consistency across views. In the RL stage, we propose a world-simulator-in-the-loop two-phase RL curriculum to stabilize tool-use exploration and advance the model's ability to invoke the simulator only when imagined observations improve over direct answering. Experiments demonstrate that both the world simulator and the agentic policy are necessary: Astra-WM improves simulator-augmented Gemini-3-Flash on MMSI-Bench from 45.1 to 49.5, while Astra-VL improves the Qwen3-VL backbone from 29.8 to 38.8 on MMSI-Bench and from 36.8 to 42.7 on MindCube. These results show that imagined observations can provide useful spatial evidence, but effective world-model-augmented reasoning requires learning when, where, and how to imagine.

ru.title: Пространственное мышление через воображение: учимся взаимодействовать с виртуальным миром
ru.desc: Astra — это агентивная система пространственного рассуждения, которая расширяет возможности моделей зрения-языка (VLM) посредством обусловленной действиями визуальной имагинации. Система состоит из двух компонентов: Astra-VL — политика VLM, обученная с помощью обучения с подкреплением, и Astra-WM — симулятор мира, генерирующий новые ракурсы наблюдений на основе контекстных изображений и текстовых команд камеры. Аstra-WM обучена с использованием настройки согласованности видов для обеспечения консистентности позы и содержания между различными точками зрения, что позволяет генерировать надежные визуальные доказательства для рассуждений. На этапе обучения с подкреплением применяется двухфазный учебный план, обучающий модель правильно определять, когда обращение к симулятору действительно улучшает качество ответов относительно прямого ответа.

en.title: Empowering Vision-Language Models with Imagination
en.desc: Astra is a new framework designed to improve the spatial reasoning abilities of Vision-Language Models (VLMs) by integrating action-conditioned visual imagination. It combines a reinforcement learning-trained policy with a world simulator to generate new visual perspectives that help the model reason about unseen layouts. The framework addresses limitations in VLMs, such as maintaining consistency across different views and reasoning from limited observations. Experiments show that Astra significantly enhances the performance of VLMs by effectively utilizing imagined visual evidence during reasoning tasks.

zh.title: Astra：增强视觉推理的想象力框架
zh.desc: Astra是一个增强视觉-语言模型的空间推理框架，通过将强化学习训练的策略与世界模拟器结合，实现基于动作的视觉想象。尽管视觉-语言模型在视觉推理方面表现出色，但它们在空间推理能力上仍然受到限制，尤其是在推断未观察到的布局和保持视角一致性方面。Astra通过与世界模拟器的交互，主动获取想象的视觉证据，从而提升推理能力。实验结果表明，想象的观察可以提供有用的空间证据，但有效的世界模型增强推理需要学习何时、何地以及如何进行想象。

papers.title: Physics in 2-Steps: Locking Motion Priors Before Visual Refinement Erases Them
papers.abstract: Abstract PhaseLock is a training-free framework that improves physical consistency in image-to-video diffusion models by preserving motion priors from early-step inference throughout the denoising process.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Image-to-Video diffusion models leverage input images to generate visually stunning content, yet frequently produce motion that violates physical laws. We reveal a surprising finding: a 2-step generation often exhibits better physical consistency than a 50-step output from the same model. Through spectral analysis, we trace this to phase erosion during denoising; the phase degrades significantly (dropping by approx 18% from step 2 to step 50), whereas the magnitude remains relatively stable. Building on this insight, we propose PhaseLock, a training-free framework that preserves the valid motion priors from few-step inference throughout the denoising trajectory. Rather than relying on full-step inference for physical consistency, PhaseLock extracts a motion prior from just 2 steps and enforces it onto high-fidelity generation via Latent Delta Guidance. Our approach effectively mitigates phase degradation, improving physical consistency by an average of 6.2 points across diverse models while largely maintaining visual fidelity, with negligible overhead (1.06times time, 1.02times memory) and reduced reliance on expensive external guidance methods (sim5times time).

ru.title: Блокировка фазы: сохранение физических законов в диффузионных видеомоделях
ru.desc: PhaseLock — это фреймворк без обучения, который улучшает физическую консистентность в диффузионных моделях преобразования изображения в видео. Авторы обнаружили, что ранние шаги генерации сохраняют более точные приоры движения, которые деградируют на протяжении процесса денойзинга из-за размывания фазы в спектре. Предложенный метод извлекает приоры движения из первых двух шагов и применяет их на весь процесс генерации через метод Latent Delta Guidance. Подход улучшает физическую согласованность видео в среднем на 6.2 пункта при минимальных вычислительных затратах и без необходимости дополнительного обучения модели.

en.title: Enhancing Physical Consistency in Video Generation with PhaseLock
en.desc: PhaseLock is a novel framework designed to enhance the physical consistency of image-to-video diffusion models without the need for extensive training. It addresses the issue of motion inconsistencies that often arise during the denoising process by preserving motion priors from early inference steps. The research reveals that using only a 2-step generation can yield better physical consistency compared to longer 50-step outputs due to phase erosion effects. By implementing Latent Delta Guidance, PhaseLock effectively maintains valid motion information throughout the denoising process, resulting in improved physical consistency while keeping visual quality intact and minimizing computational costs.

zh.title: PhaseLock：提升图像到视频生成的物理一致性
zh.desc: PhaseLock是一个无需训练的框架，旨在提高图像到视频扩散模型的物理一致性。它通过在去噪过程中保持早期推理的运动先验，来改善生成内容的物理合理性。研究发现，2步生成的物理一致性往往优于50步生成，原因在于去噪过程中的相位侵蚀现象。PhaseLock通过提取少量步骤的运动先验，并将其应用于高保真生成，有效减轻了相位降解，提升了物理一致性，同时保持了视觉质量。

papers.title: LLM Explainability with Counterfactual Chains and Causal Graphs
papers.abstract: Abstract Causal graphs are used to model large language model inference processes, enabling transparent visualization of how models perceive and organize high-level concepts for predictions through a four-phase method involving concept discovery, mapping, and MCMC-inspired counterfactual augmentation.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Causal graphs provide a high-level language for making mechanisms transparent. Recent work uses Large Language Models (LLMs) to recover causal graphs of external-world processes. Instead, in this paper, we use causal graphs to model LLM inference itself, providing stakeholders with a transparent view of how the model perceives and organizes high-level concepts to produce a prediction. We propose a four-phase method for constructing such graphs. Given a target LLM and a set of textual examples, our method discovers class-discriminative, human-interpretable concepts and maps each input to LLM-perceived concept states. We then introduce an MCMC-inspired counterfactual augmentation procedure that expands the sparse observational data through chains of counterfactuals. This enables stable causal discovery with σ-CG, yielding informative, interpretable graphs. We apply our method to three LLMs across disease diagnosis, sentiment analysis, and LLM-as-a-judge classification tasks. We evaluate the learned graphs for predictive fidelity and structural stability, and the MCMC-inspired augmentation for convergence and downstream utility. Our results show that the discovered causal graphs capture meaningful dependencies consistent with LLMs' reasoning. Together, this paper provides a foundation for concept-level explainability of LLMs.
affiliations: Faculty of Data and Decision Sciences, Technion

ru.title: Причинные графы для интерпретируемости больших языковых моделей на уровне концепций
ru.desc: В статье предлагается метод построения причинных графов для визуализации процесса вывода в больших языковых моделях, что позволяет понять, как модели воспринимают и организуют высокоуровневые концепции при принятии решений. Авторы разработали четырёхэтапный подход, включающий обнаружение интерпретируемых концепций, их отображение на входные данные и применение MCMC-вдохновленной процедуры для генерации контрфактических примеров, расширяющей обучающий набор. Метод использует алгоритм причинного открытия σ-CG для построения стабильных и информативных графов, которые отражают действительные логические зависимости в рассуждениях LLM. Экспериментальная оценка на трёх моделях и различных задачах показала эффективность подхода в достижении предсказательной точности и структурной устойчивости графов.

en.title: Unveiling LLMs: Causal Graphs for Transparent Predictions
en.desc: This paper introduces a method to use causal graphs for understanding how large language models (LLMs) make predictions. It outlines a four-phase approach that includes discovering concepts, mapping inputs to these concepts, and using a counterfactual augmentation technique inspired by Markov Chain Monte Carlo (MCMC) to enhance data. The goal is to create interpretable graphs that reveal how LLMs perceive and organize information, making their reasoning processes more transparent. The method is tested on various tasks, demonstrating that the causal graphs effectively capture the relationships and dependencies in the model's predictions.

zh.title: 因果图：揭示大型语言模型推理的透明视角
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种使用因果图来建模大型语言模型（LLM）推理过程的方法。通过四个阶段的流程，首先发现可区分的概念，然后将输入映射到模型感知的概念状态，最后通过MCMC启发的反事实增强程序扩展观察数据。该方法使得因果关系的发现更加稳定，并生成可解释的因果图。实验结果表明，所发现的因果图能够有效捕捉与LLM推理一致的有意义依赖关系。

papers.title: UnpredictaBench: A Benchmark for Evaluating Distributional Randomness in LLMs
papers.abstract: Abstract UnpredictaBench evaluates large language models' capacity to sample from target distributions, revealing significant gaps in their ability to simulate unpredictable systems despite recent advances in output diversity.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct We introduce UnpredictaBench, an evaluation that tests the ability of large language models (LLMs) to capture true underlying distributions. As LLMs are increasingly used as substitutes for other entities (e.g., for humans in economic simulations), the tendency of many models to collapse towards a single plausible answer means a failure to capture the unpredictability of real systems. Recent work on improving output diversity is insufficient for this setting: simulation requires samples that are calibrated to a target distribution, not merely varied outputs. UnpredictaBench isolates a simplified but fundamental version of this problem: sampling outcomes from individual target distributions, including canonical statistical distributions, distributions induced by stochastic programs, and natural-language scenarios that describe random processes. We introduce 448 such problems together with KS@N, a general-purpose evaluation metric that quantifies how well a model outputs approximate black-box target distributions via the Kolmogorov-Smirnov statistical test. This is the rate at which we fail to reject model samples of size N against ground-truth samples, with larger N indicating greater difficulty. Tested across open and proprietary models, we find a large spread in distributional capabilities. For instance, when models generate samples of size 100 (KS@100, our standard metric), scores range from near 0 to over 20%. No model is able to achieve over 40% at KS@100, showing significant headroom in distributional sampling as a capability. Although adding reasoning can somewhat increase scores, we find no immediate solution for this issue. UnpredictaBench shows that even simple distributional simulation remains challenging, making it a necessary first step toward using LLMs as stand-ins for complex systems.

ru.title: Моделирование вероятностей: задача, которая остаётся сложной даже для современных LLM
ru.desc: В статье представлен UnpredictaBench — инструмент для оценки способности больших языковых моделей выбирать выборки из целевых распределений вероятностей. Авторы показывают, что современные LLM имеют значительные пробелы в моделировании непредсказуемых систем, поскольку они часто коллапсируют в единственный правдоподобный ответ вместо того, чтобы отражать истинное распределение. Бенчмарк включает 448 задач с различными типами распределений и использует метрику KS@N на основе теста Колмогорова-Смирнова для количественной оценки качества аппроксимации. Результаты показывают, что ни одна модель не может достичь более 40% точности при KS@100, что свидетельствует о значительном потенциале для улучшения в области вероятностной выборки.

en.title: UnpredictaBench: Bridging the Gap in Language Model Sampling
en.desc: UnpredictaBench is a new evaluation framework designed to assess how well large language models (LLMs) can sample from true target distributions. Despite improvements in output diversity, many LLMs struggle to simulate the unpredictability found in real-world systems, often defaulting to a single plausible answer. The framework introduces 448 distinct problems and uses the KS@N metric to measure how closely model outputs match the desired distributions. Results indicate that current models have significant limitations in their distributional capabilities, highlighting the need for further advancements in this area.

zh.title: 评估语言模型的分布采样能力
zh.desc: UnpredictaBench 是一个评估大型语言模型（LLMs）从目标分布中采样能力的工具。尽管最近在输出多样性方面取得了一些进展，但许多模型仍然倾向于收敛到单一的合理答案，无法有效模拟真实系统的不可预测性。该评估引入了448个问题，并使用 KS@N 作为通用评估指标，量化模型输出与真实样本的接近程度。研究发现，当前模型在分布采样能力上存在显著差距，表明在将 LLMs 用作复杂系统的替代品之前，仍需解决分布模拟的挑战。

papers.title: PaperFlow: Profiling, Recommending, and Adapting Across Daily Paper Streams
papers.abstract: Abstract PaperFlow is a framework for scientific paper recommendation that processes user profiles, daily paper streams, and interest drift through three stages: profiling, recommending, and adapting, using a longitudinal benchmark with 24 users, 50 daily streams, and 1,200 episodes.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Scientific paper recommendation is typically evaluated as static ranking over a fixed candidate set, yet real scientific reading unfolds as a daily, longitudinal process in which interests shift and feedback accumulates. We introduce PaperFlow, a framework that organizes it into three coupled stages: Profiling, which constructs and maintains a structured, inspectable scholarly profile from heterogeneous cold-start evidence; Recommending, which ranks each date-specific paper stream through multi-signal aggregation under a fixed display budget; and Adapting, which updates user state from semantically distinct feedback signals and models interest drift across days. We further define a longitudinal user-day benchmark that fixes users, dates, candidate pools, visible inputs, and hidden simulated relevance labels under a shared temporal information boundary. The benchmark contains 24 simulated research users, 50 daily paper streams, 1,200 user-day episodes, 20,727 unique papers, and 497,448 episode-paper records. We additionally specify a blind human-evaluation protocol to validate alignment between automatic metrics and expert judgments. Experiments against five scientific recommendation baselines show that PaperFlow achieves the strongest oracle-based ranking, the highest behavioral alignment with simulated reading selections, and the best blind human-evaluation score.
affiliations: Key Laboratory of Computing Power Network and Information Security, Ministry of Education, Shandong Computer Science Center (National Supercomputer Center in Jinan), Qilu University of Technology (Shandong Academy of Sciences)
University of Chinese Academy of Science

ru.title: Адаптивная рекомендация научных статей с учётом эволюции интересов
ru.desc: PaperFlow — это фреймворк для рекомендации научных статей, который моделирует процесс научного чтения как продольное исследование с тремя связанными этапами: построение профиля пользователя, ранжирование ежедневных потоков статей и адаптация к изменению интересов. Авторы предложили новый подход к оценке рекомендательных систем, заменив статическую оценку на фиксированном наборе на реалистичный сценарий с динамическим изменением предпочтений пользователей во времени. Они создали продольный бенчмарк с 24 симулированными пользователями, 50 ежедневными потоками и 1200 эпизодами, содержащий более 20 тысяч уникальных статей. Экспериментальные результаты показывают, что PaperFlow превосходит пять базовых методов по метрикам ранжирования, соответствию поведению пользователей и оценкам экспертов.

en.title: Dynamic Recommendations for Evolving Scientific Interests
en.desc: PaperFlow is a novel framework designed for recommending scientific papers by adapting to user interests over time. It operates in three main stages: profiling user preferences, recommending papers based on daily streams, and adapting to changes in user interests through feedback. Unlike traditional methods that treat recommendations as static, PaperFlow recognizes that user interests can evolve, requiring a dynamic approach to paper selection. The framework has been rigorously tested with a comprehensive benchmark, demonstrating superior performance in aligning recommendations with actual user behavior and expert evaluations.

zh.title: PaperFlow：动态科学论文推荐的新框架
zh.desc: PaperFlow是一个科学论文推荐框架，旨在处理用户档案、每日论文流和兴趣漂移。它通过三个阶段：档案构建、推荐和适应，来组织推荐过程。该框架使用了一个包含24个用户、50个每日论文流和1200个用户-日期事件的长期基准。实验结果表明，PaperFlow在自动评估指标和专家判断之间具有良好的对齐性，并在科学推荐基准中表现出色。

papers.title: Socratic-SWE: Self-Evolving Coding Agents via Trace-Derived Agent Skills
papers.abstract: Abstract Socratic-SWE enables self-evolving software engineering agents by leveraging historical solving traces to generate targeted repair tasks that improve agent performance through iterative refinement.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct LLM-driven software engineering agents have become a central testbed for real-world language-model capability, yet their training remains limited by the availability of high-quality SWE tasks. Existing synthetic data methods typically create tasks through fixed mutation or bug-injection procedures, making the resulting distributions largely independent of the agent's own weaknesses and training progress. We introduce Socratic-SWE, a closed-loop self-evolution framework that reuses the agent's historical solving traces as a source of training signal. Rather than treating traces only as evidence for reward computation, Socratic-SWE distills them into structured agent skills that summarize recurring failures and effective repair patterns. These skills then guide the generation of targeted repair tasks in real repositories. Candidate tasks are checked through execution-based validation and scored with a solver-gradient alignment reward, so that the retained tasks are both verifiable and useful for improving the Solver. The updated Solver produces new traces, enabling the task curriculum to adapt over successive rounds. Across SWE-bench Verified, SWE-bench Lite, SWE-bench Pro, and Terminal-Bench 2.0, Socratic-SWE consistently improves over self-evolving baselines under the same compute budget, reaching 50.40% on SWE-bench Verified after three iterations. These results suggest that solving traces can serve as a scalable substrate for self-evolving SWE agents.

ru.title: Самоэволюция агентов через переработку собственного опыта решения
ru.desc: Socratic-SWE представляет закрытый цикл самоэволюции для агентов инженерии программного обеспечения, которые улучшают свою производительность через итеративное обучение. Система анализирует исторические логи решений агента, извлекает структурированные навыки из повторяющихся ошибок и эффективных паттернов исправления. На основе этих навыков генерируются целевые задачи восстановления в реальных репозиториях, которые валидируются через выполнение кода и оцениваются специальной метрикой выравнивания. Благодаря адаптивной очередности задач агент достигает значительного улучшения производительности, демонстрируя, что логи решений являются масштабируемым ресурсом для самообучения.

en.title: Empowering Self-Evolving Agents Through Historical Learning
en.desc: Socratic-SWE is a framework designed to enhance the performance of software engineering agents by using their past solving experiences to create specific repair tasks. Instead of relying on traditional methods that generate tasks through fixed mutations, this approach utilizes historical traces to identify common failures and effective solutions. By distilling these traces into structured skills, the framework generates targeted tasks that are validated through execution, ensuring they are both practical and beneficial for the agent's improvement. This iterative process allows the agent to continuously evolve and adapt its learning based on its own performance, leading to significant advancements in task-solving capabilities.

zh.title: 自我进化的软件工程代理新方法
zh.desc: Socratic-SWE 是一种自我进化的软件工程代理框架，通过利用历史解决轨迹生成针对性的修复任务，从而提高代理的性能。该方法不同于传统的固定变异或注入错误的合成数据方法，而是将历史轨迹提炼为结构化的代理技能，帮助识别常见的失败和有效的修复模式。生成的修复任务经过执行验证，并通过求解器梯度对齐奖励进行评分，以确保任务的可验证性和实用性。实验结果表明，Socratic-SWE 在多个基准测试中表现优异，证明了解决轨迹可以作为自我进化软件工程代理的可扩展基础。

papers.title: SIA: Self Improving AI with Harness & Weight Updates
papers.abstract: Abstract A self-improving AI framework simultaneously updates both model weights and task-specific agent architecture through a language-model feedback agent across legal classification, GPU optimization, and biological data denoising tasks.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Humans are the bottleneck in building and improving AI. Both the models and the agents that wrap them are written, tuned, and corrected by people. The long-horizon goal of an AI that can figure out how to improve itself remains open. Two largely disjoint research lines attack this bottleneck. The harness-update school has a meta-agent rewrite the scaffold of a task-specific agent (its tools, prompts, retry logic, and search procedure) while the model weights are held fixed. The test-time training school uses hand-written RL pipelines to update the model's own weights on task feedback while the harness is held fixed. These two silos operate in isolation. We propose SIA, a self-improving loop in which a language-model agent (the Feedback-Agent) updates both the harness and the weights of a task-specific agent. We evaluate across three contrasting domains: Chinese legal charge classification, low-level GPU kernel optimisation, and single-cell RNA denoising. Combining both levers outperforms scaffold iteration alone on all three benchmarks. The gains are 56.6% on LawBench, 91.9% runtime reduction on GPU kernels, and 502% on denoising over the initial baseline. Harness updates make the model agentic, shaping how it searches and acts, while weight updates build the domain intuition that no prompt or scaffold can instil.

ru.title: Двойное совершенствование: одновременное обновление весов и архитектуры агента
ru.desc: В работе предлагается фреймворк SIA для самоулучшения AI, где языковая модель одновременно обновляет как веса основной модели, так и архитектуру task-specific агента на основе своей собственной обратной связи. Авторы объединяют два существующих подхода: обновление архитектуры агента (harness-update) и обновление весов модели (test-time training), которые ранее развивались отдельно. Фреймворк оценивается на трёх разнородных задачах: классификация китайских судебных обвинений, оптимизация GPU ядер и денойзинг данных одиночных клеток. Комбинированный подход значительно превосходит базовые методы, достигая улучшений на 56.6%, 91.9% и 502% соответственно на разных задачах.

en.title: Empowering AI: Self-Improvement through Integrated Learning
en.desc: This paper introduces a self-improving AI framework called SIA, which enhances both the model weights and the architecture of task-specific agents using feedback from a language model. It addresses the limitations of traditional approaches that either focus on updating the agent's structure or the model's weights in isolation. By integrating these two processes, SIA demonstrates significant performance improvements across various tasks, including legal classification, GPU optimization, and biological data denoising. The results show that this combined approach leads to substantial gains in efficiency and accuracy, highlighting the potential for AI systems to autonomously enhance their capabilities.

zh.title: 自我改进的AI框架：双重更新，性能提升
zh.desc: 这篇论文提出了一种自我改进的人工智能框架，能够同时更新模型权重和任务特定的代理架构。通过语言模型反馈代理，该框架在法律分类、GPU优化和生物数据去噪等任务中进行评估。研究表明，结合这两种更新方式的效果优于单独的架构迭代，分别在三个基准测试中取得了显著的性能提升。该方法使模型具备自主性，能够更好地进行搜索和行动，同时通过权重更新增强了领域直觉。

papers.title: SPACENUM: Revisiting Spatial Numerical Understanding in VLMs
papers.abstract: Abstract Vision-language models struggle to genuinely understand spatial numerical concepts, relying instead on shallow visual cues rather than developing robust coordinate-aware representations.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Vision-Language Models (VLMs) are increasingly deployed in embodied environments, where they need produce numerical outputs such as action magnitudes and spatial coordinates. Although these numbers appear meaningful, it remains unclear whether these numerical outputs are genuinely grounded in spatial perception. Therefore, in this work, we revisit spatial numerical understanding through SpaceNum, a unified framework that captures two complementary settings: numbers as dynamic transitions during spatial exploration, and numbers as static layouts in spatial reasoning. We formulate two bidirectional tasks, Num2Space and Space2Num, to evaluate how well VLMs map between vision-side spatial structure and language-side numerical representations. We systematically study whether current VLMs truly understand numerical values in spatial settings. Across dynamic transitions and static layouts, we find that models largely fail to ground numbers in spatial meaning and often perform close to random guess. Through error analysis, reasoning trace analysis, and controlled interventions, we show that current VLMs rely heavily on shallow spatial cues, struggle to build stable coordinate-aware representations, and fail to abstract structured spatial layouts from visual observations. We further show that explicit reasoning provides only marginal gains, while tuning can partially improve spatial numerical understanding and transfer to external spatial reasoning benchmarks.

ru.title: Мультимодальные модели не понимают пространство и числа в единстве
ru.desc: В работе исследуется проблема понимания пространственных числовых концепций в мультимодальных моделях vision-language. Авторы предлагают фреймворк SpaceNum, который оценивает способность моделей устанавливать соответствие между визуальными пространственными структурами и числовыми представлениями через две взаимные задачи: Num2Space и Space2Num. Результаты показывают, что современные VLM-модели не в состоянии правильно заземлить числовые значения в пространственном контексте, вместо этого полагаясь на поверхностные визуальные признаки и не формируя стабильные координатные представления. Исследование демонстрирует, что явное рассуждение дает лишь незначительные улучшения, но fine-tuning может частично повысить пространственное числовое понимание и улучшить результаты на внешних бенчмарках.

en.title: Bridging Vision and Numbers: Understanding Spatial Context in VLMs
en.desc: This paper investigates how well vision-language models (VLMs) understand spatial numerical concepts. It introduces SpaceNum, a framework that examines two scenarios: numbers as dynamic changes during spatial exploration and as fixed layouts in spatial reasoning. The authors propose two tasks, Num2Space and Space2Num, to assess the models' ability to connect visual spatial structures with numerical representations. The findings reveal that current VLMs often fail to accurately ground numerical values in spatial contexts, relying instead on superficial visual cues and struggling to form stable, coordinate-aware representations.

zh.title: 提升视觉语言模型的空间数值理解能力
zh.desc: 本文探讨了视觉语言模型在理解空间数值概念方面的不足。这些模型往往依赖于表面的视觉线索，而不是建立稳健的坐标感知表示。我们提出了SpaceNum框架，通过两个双向任务Num2Space和Space2Num来评估模型在视觉空间结构与语言数值表示之间的映射能力。研究表明，当前的视觉语言模型在空间设置中对数值的理解存在显著缺陷，表现接近随机猜测。

papers.title: Stream3D-VLM: Online 3D Spatial Understanding with Incremental Geometry Priors
papers.abstract: Abstract An online 3D vision-language model enables real-time spatial understanding from streaming video using autoregressive control modeling and efficient visual token compression.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Despite advances in 3D scene understanding, existing 3D Large Multimodal Models operate in offline settings, requiring complete scene observations or predefined video clips. In this paper, we present an online 3D vision-language model that enables real-time spatial understanding from streaming video. Our approach adopts an autoregressive streaming control modeling based on the LLM's next-token prediction objective to learn when to respond, and employs a lightweight Visual-Spatial Feature Integration (VSFI) module to incrementally inject temporally aligned geometry priors into the visual stream. To alleviate long-context decoding overhead, we propose a plug-and-play Geometry-Adaptive Voxel Compression (GAVC) module for efficient visual token compression. To address the scarcity of streaming 3D-language data, we further develop a scalable data generation pipeline that curates over 1M online spatio-temporal 3D QA pairs and establishes a comprehensive benchmark spanning 29 tasks. Extensive experiments show that our approach significantly outperforms both proprietary and open-source models across online and offline 3D spatial understanding, reasoning, and grounding tasks. The project page is available at https://stream3d-vlm.github.io/

ru.title: Трёхмерное видение и язык в реальном времени из потокового видео
ru.desc: В работе представлена онлайн-модель трёхмерного зрения и языка, которая обеспечивает понимание пространственных сцен в режиме реального времени из потокового видео.
